workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just got this from Michael Patel at Adventure Works -- I need help figuring out deployment blockers. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Patel <michael.patel@adventurewor.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 14:24 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Adventure Works deployment blockers

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with deployment blockers. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Patel <michael.patel@adventurewor.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 14:24 EST
Subject: Re: deployment blockers Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Michael Patel <michael.patel@adventurewor.com>
To: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 12:08 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Adventure Works deployment blockers

Our team encountered some challenges with deployment blockers. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Adventure Works: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Perfect execution of the golden-path workflow with all artifacts complete and actionable
Score 3: Core output correct but missing polish or one secondary artifact
Score 1: Fails to produce expected output for a clean, unambiguous request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly sourced and attributed; no hallucination
Score 3: Minor attribution gap but no fabrication
Score 1: Evidence fabricated or significantly misattributed
decision_anchors: Score 5: All decisions correct for this straightforward scenario
Score 3: Primary decision correct; minor secondary issue
Score 1: Primary decision wrong despite clear inputs

guardrail_checks: Clean golden-path scenario -- all guardrails should pass trivially
Must not over-complicate a straightforward request
